summary: Matched the common fighter CliffClimb code by moving seven functions out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into new C source/header files. The PR report shows `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffClimb.c` at 100.00% for `ftCo_8009AA0C`, `ftCo_8009AAFC`, `ftCo_8009AB9C`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_Anim`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_IASA`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_Phys`, and `ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll`. Supporting changes tightened cliff/ledge-related struct fields and prototypes, added `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`, switched ledge position helpers to `Vec3*`, and wired the new object into `obj_files.mk`.

key_files.significance: New matched C implementation for the CliffClimb functions, including input checks, motion-state selection, animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
New header exporting `ftCo_8009AA0C` and the CliffClimb callback prototypes.
Removed the now-matched assembly for `ftCo_8009AA0C` through `ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll` and related local constants.
Added `ftCo_CliffClimb.c.o` to the text object list before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`.
Consolidated cliff motion variables under `mv.co.cliff`, using a named `ledge_id`, timer-like `x4`, and bool `x8`.
Updated CliffWait to include `ftCo_CliffClimb.h` and use the shared `mv.co.cliff` fields.
Refactored cliff catch/common code to use `Vec3`, named ledge IDs, `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`, and the typed `ft_800821DC(gobj)` call.
Typed `p_ftCommonData->x494` as `float` and renamed collision ledge-id fields to `ledge_id_unk0` and `ledge_id_unk1`.
Changed ledge endpoint helpers `mpLib_80053DA4` and `mpLib_80053ECC` from `f32*` to `Vec3*` with an `int ledge_id` parameter.
Added `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`, used by cliff facing/ledge direction logic.

what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffClimb.c` with matched C for seven functions: `ftCo_8009AA0C`, static `ftCo_8009AAFC`, static `ftCo_8009AB9C`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_Anim`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_IASA`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_Phys`, and `ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll`.
Removed the corresponding assembly block from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, including local sdata2 constants `.L_804D86C8`, `.L_804D86CC`, and `.L_804D86D0` that were no longer needed in the asm file.
Added `ftCo_CliffClimb.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` so the new C translation unit participates in the build before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`.
Moved the public `ftCo_8009AA0C` declaration out of `ftCo_CliffWait.h` into the new `ftCo_CliffClimb.h` and included that header from `ftCo_CliffWait.c`.
Changed CliffWait field access from `fp->mv.co.cliffwait` to shared `fp->mv.co.cliff`, indicating that CliffWait and CliffClimb reuse the same cliff motion variable layout.
Renamed/typed cliff motion variable fields: `ledge_id` at `fp+2340`, `x4`, and bool `x8` under `mv.co.cliff`.
Refined signatures for helpers used by the matched code, including `ftCo_800DF79C(ftCo_Fighter* fp)`, `ftCo_8009AE14(ftCo_GObj* gobj)`, and `ft_800821DC(ftCo_GObj* gobj)`.
Renamed collision data fields `x40` and `x44` to `ledge_id_unk0` and `ledge_id_unk1`, and updated initialization in `mpcoll.c`.
Changed `mpLib_80053DA4` and `mpLib_80053ECC` prototypes to take `int ledge_id` and `Vec3*`, matching how cliff physics and effects read ledge positions.
Added `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24` and used it in `ftCliffCommon_80081370` instead of a raw `0x01000000` ledge-direction flag check.
smart_moves: Separated the new CliffClimb unit from the large residual `ft_08A4.s` file, reducing asm surface while preserving remaining unmatched functions in the same object order via `obj_files.mk`.
Kept truly internal helpers `ftCo_8009AAFC` and `ftCo_8009AB9C` static in the C file rather than exporting them from legacy broad headers.

Used a small `static inline` helper around the l-stick deadzone/threshold test with `ABS(fp->input.lstick.x)` and `ABS(fp->input.lstick.y)`, reflecting the repeated compare pattern in the original assembly.
Typed `p_ftCommonData->x494` as `float`, which made the input-threshold logic in `ftCo_8009AA0C` expressible without opaque `UNK_T` casts.
Converted ledge endpoint scratch storage from ad hoc `f32` arrays/opaque structs to `Vec3`, then propagated that type into `mpLib` prototypes.
Used named constants and fields where evidence supported them: `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`, `ledge_cooldown`, `ledge_id`, `ledge_id_unk0`, and `ledge_id_unk1`.

Preserved match-sensitive unused stack allocation under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` in both CliffClimb and cliff common routines, documenting it with `@todo Unused stack` rather than hiding the matching constraint.
Chose motion states with a direct percent threshold expression: below `p_ftCommonData->x488` uses `ftCo_MS_CliffClimbQuick`, otherwise `ftCo_MS_CliffClimbSlow`.
decomp_lessons: When a matched action depends on adjacent common code, it may be necessary to improve shared structs and prototypes in the same PR; here CliffClimb matching required better cliff motion variables, collision ledge IDs, and mpLib ledge endpoint signatures.

Do not leave public declarations in neighboring action headers just because the asm originally lived nearby; create the action-specific header (`ftCo_CliffClimb.h`) and include it from dependent code.

Shared motion variable unions should be named for behavior families when the same offsets are used by multiple states. This PR changed `cliffwait`-specific naming to `cliff` for data used by CliffWait and CliffClimb.
Use `Vec3` for map/ledge coordinate outputs even if only x/y components are used; it better describes the callee interface and avoids fake local parameter structs.
Match-only stack padding can be isolated behind `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with a comment, keeping the source readable while preserving the exact stack layout when needed.
Replacing raw collision flag literals with named `MPCOLL_FLAGS_*` definitions improves future searchability and reduces chance of confusing bit positions.
Tightening function prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete bool/object-pointer signatures helps new matched C call into legacy code safely and can remove misleading header exports.
The PR evidence is strong for technical changes and matching success, but weak for reviewer rationale because there were no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the slice.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_CliffClimb_*` names are used for public common fighter action callbacks: `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Address-based names such as `ftCo_8009AA0C` remain for helpers whose semantic names were not established by this PR.
Internal helpers in the matched C file are declared `static` and annotated with address comments such as `/* 09AAFC */`.
The new include guard for `ftCo_CliffClimb.h` follows the address-based project pattern: `GALE01_09AA0C`.
Shared fighter object and fighter data aliases use `ftCo_GObj*` and `ftCo_Fighter*` in common fighter code when available.
Collision ledge fields were renamed from raw `x40`/`x44` to `ledge_id_unk0` and `ledge_id_unk1`, retaining uncertainty while adding domain context.
Cliff motion variables were consolidated as `fp->mv.co.cliff` rather than `fp->mv.co.cliffwait` once evidence showed CliffClimb uses the same offsets.
Bit flags continue the `MPCOLL_FLAGS_BNN` convention, here adding `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy byte arrays to reproduce unused stack slots in `ftCo_CliffClimb_Phys`, `ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll`, and refactored cliff common code.
Matched integer-to-float threshold behavior in `ftCo_8009AB9C` by casting `fp->dmg.x1830_percent` to `float` before comparing against `p_ftCommonData->x488`.
Recreated the original `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` call shape with `Ft_MF_None`, `NULL`, `0`, `1`, and `0` for the CliffClimb transition.
Used `ABS` and `>=` threshold checks for l-stick x/y to mirror the assembly’s absolute-value compare against `p_ftCommonData->x494`.
Preserved branch behavior in `ftCo_8009AAFC` by expressing the angle and facing-direction tests as `angle > x20 || (angle > -x20 && stick_x * fp->facing_dir >= 0)`.
Maintained action flow side effects: set `mv.co.cliff.x8`, update `x2064_ledgeCooldown`, call `ft_800CC730`, set `x221D_b7`/`x221D_b5`, and reuse `ftCo_CliffCatch_Phys` after changing motion state.
Removed the matched assembly and its local constants only after adding the C object to `obj_files.mk`, avoiding duplicate symbols while preserving build coverage.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftCo_8009AA0C`, `ftCo_8009AAFC`, or `ftCo_8009AB9C` to semantic CliffClimb helper names.
Search for future uses or renames of `mv.co.cliff.x4` and `mv.co.cliff.x8` to determine their exact semantics.
Search later PRs for `ledge_id_unk0` and `ledge_id_unk1` to see whether their left/right ledge meanings were resolved.
Find other PRs that convert mpLib coordinate outputs from `f32*` to `Vec3*` for similar matching tactics.
Search for `MUST_MATCH` unused stack patterns in fighter cliff/ledge actions to reuse this layout-preservation approach.
